issue_title: [Bug]: UUID validation fails for uppercase UUIDs

analysis.summary: The `uuid`, `uuid3`, `uuid4`, and `uuid5` validation tags reject valid UUIDs that contain uppercase hex digits (A-F). The regex strings for these four validators only allow lowercase hex characters (`[0-9a-f]`), while the corresponding `_rfc4122` variants correctly use `[0-9a-fA-F]`. The documentation in doc.go even acknowledges this as intentional, but the GitHub issue flags it as a bug since UUIDs are case-insensitive by specification.
analysis.root_cause: In `regexes.go`, the four constants `uUIDRegexString`, `uUID3RegexString`, `uUID4RegexString`, and `uUID5RegexString` use the character class `[0-9a-f]` (lowercase only), while the RFC4122 counterparts correctly use `[0-9a-fA-F]`. Additionally, `uUID4RegexString` and `uUID5RegexString` use `[89ab]` for the variant nibble instead of `[89abAB]`. This causes the `uuid`, `uuid3`, `uuid4`, and `uuid5` validators to reject uppercase UUIDs.

relevant_files.why: Lines 33-36 define the four broken regex constants that must be updated to include `A-F` (and `AB` for variant nibble in uuid4/uuid5).
Lines 1145, 1151, 1157, and 1163 document 'Uppercase UUID values will not pass' — these notes must be removed once the fix is applied.
TestUUIDValidation, TestUUID3Validation, TestUUID4Validation, TestUUID5Validation need new test cases with uppercase UUID strings to cover the fixed behaviour.

plan: In `doc.go`, update the descriptions for `uuid`, `uuid3`, `uuid4`, and `uuid5` to remove the 'Uppercase UUID values will not pass' caveat (since they now accept uppercase).

In `validator_test.go`, add uppercase UUID test cases (expected: true) to TestUUIDValidation, TestUUID3Validation, TestUUID4Validation, and TestUUID5Validation — e.g. `5ADE1109-1E12-4E46-8E41-EF204C423153` for uuid, matching the format of existing RFC4122 test cases.
validation: go test ./... (all existing tests must still pass)
